subcodes.title: Words to Mary Hanford Ford in 1910
subcodes.first_line: I think we should learn to live in the body as if it were a glass case, through which we can look clearly on all sides. But we must remember that we cannot see through glass unless it is clean, and no one can dust his own case but himself.
subcodes.subjects: @occult sciences; psychic phenomena, @self-improvement; self-perfection; discipline, @spiritual transformation
